rule: PG bilingual volumes duplicate column numbers across languages (Greek leaf and Latin leaf both carry e.g. 531-532) and the language side alternates per spread (G,L then L,G). Classify every leaf by language+col-range; never assume alternation parity.

Gemini 2.5 Flash with thinkingBudget:0 + temperature:0 can enter repetition runaway on column crops (not on full pages). Detect: candidatesTokenCount > 2x median of sibling calls -> discard and fall back to full-page pass or rerun with thinking on.

In bilingual volumes the parallel translation is a free error detector: diff proper nouns and section structure across Greek/Latin pairs before zoom-verification (e.g. Greek 'Appamias' vs Latin 'Appiariae' exposes one side as wrong).
evidence: idx152 col1 vs idx151 col1 episcopal sees

rule: Verification taxonomy must be 3-way, not binary: GEMINI-ERROR (fix transcript) / MIGNE-TYPO (keep + [sic] + emendation log) / OK. A diplomatic transcript that silently fixes print typos destroys the scholarly value; one that keeps OCR errors destroys accuracy.
evidence: cousin task requires typo-notorious Migne edition with typos preserved and flagged

rule: Cheap scholar-pass over the raw transcript (grammatical impossibilities, dangling cases, cross-language disagreements, e/c u/n in/m Latin compositor classes) yields a suspect list that focuses Opus verification; pass it in the verifier prompt.
evidence: ~25 suspects extracted from 4 transcripts before any zooming

rule: OCR errors MIMIC compositor error classes (e/c, in/m, dropped letters): 'looks like a classic 19th-c print typo' is zero evidence the print is at fault. Every typo-candidate must be zoom-adjudicated; of ~6 'Migne typo' suspects, 4 were Gemini errors over clean print.
evidence: perdueit/subjungiur/exagitatuin/negotia-rum all GEMINI-ERROR (idx149); super haec re GEMINI-ERROR (idx151); only pestringeret (idx150) + Co.slinianus glyph (idx149) were real print anomalies

rule: Canonical-text benchmark accuracy does NOT transfer to less-famous text: error rate on Chrysostom letters ~20/Greek page vs ~1/page on the Beatitudes homilies (same model, same config). Budget verification effort by text fame, not by benchmark CER.
evidence: idx152: 22 gemini errors; PG57 p150 benchmark: near-zero in body

rule: Hyphenation line-skips are a silent OCR failure class: a swallowed hyphen-head turns 'Cardi-/nali' into 'nali' and 'po-/puli' into 'puli'. Detect: orphan word-fragments after a line boundary; verify the preceding line end.
evidence: idx150 two line-skips found by verifier

rule: Apparatus note letters (a,b,...h,i,j,k) are a sigla class like superscript digits: models confuse i/j and duplicate letters. Always verify the note-letter sequence is strictly increasing with no repeats.
evidence: idx152 apparatus has h,i,j,k; Gemini produced two k's and read j as i

rule: Pre-cutting the strip grid by script (instead of letting verifier agents crop their own) collapses verifier loop length: 13-18 tool calls vs 45-82, and tokens drop ~42% (73K/page vs 126K/page). The cost of agentic verification is loop length, not model intelligence.
evidence: run2 Sonnet verifiers: 62-84K tokens, 158-341s; run1 Opus: 113-155K, 527-1117s

rule: Sonnet replaces Opus as the verification tier when given pre-cut native-res strips + a mechanical divergence worklist; reserve Opus/orchestrator for escalations only. Escalation rate observed: 1 item per 5 pages, resolved by orchestrator with 3 tool calls + lexical corroboration.
evidence: run2: 103 fixes, 4 [sic], 1 escalation (Kampidouktor) across 5 pages, no quality regressions found at stitch

rule: Buying TWO cheap OCR passes (full-page + per-column) and diffing them programmatically is a free error detector: 36-85 divergences/page, zero tokens. Structural ordering noise is filterable; repetition-runaway tails detectable by length cap.
Apparatus blocks print FULL-WIDTH while body text is two-column: column crops split apparatus lines mid-line. Always cut a separate full-width bottom strip for apparatus verification.
evidence: strips/idxNNN_app.png added after run1 colL apparatus errors

rule: A single verifier pass leaves ~2-3 residual errors/page that look verified. The final quality gate for scholar-grade output is CROSS-MODEL ENSEMBLE DISAGREEMENT: run a second engine (different family) over the finished pages, diff against the verified text, adjudicate every dispute by eye. Verifier errors and spurious [sic] flags only surface this way.
evidence: 3-flash-preview vs verified idx153/156: 9 disputes adjudicated, 5 went AGAINST the verified text (krinantes, mekei, esmen-no-accent un-flagging a published [sic], dative plurals, akairon)

rule: Engine choice updated: gemini-3-flash-preview supersedes 2.5-flash for the bulk pass (5x fewer error-words on the hard Greek page, faster, similar cost; thinkingBudget:0 accepted, no repetition runaways observed in 9 calls). gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview is NOT better than 2.5-flash. Caveat: 3-flash has stronger canonical priors -> verification matters more, not less.
evidence: A/B on idx153/156 scored vs adjudicated ground truth; 3.1-lite at or below baseline on both pages

rule: Verifier errors run in BOTH directions: inventing print anomalies that aren't there (spurious [sic]) AND erasing real print anomalies by over-correcting them into clean text (exagitatuin -> exagitatum). The ensemble gate is the only stage that catches over-correction, because a second engine reading the same print reproduces the anomaly independently.
evidence: idx156 esmen [sic] refuted; idx149 exagitatuin restored as genuine Migne typo after Opus verifier had 'fixed' it

rule: Watch for double-rho with breathing (ἀνεῤῥίπισε, ἀποῤῥήξας) and similar 19th-c Greek print conventions: verifiers and diff normalization both tend to flatten them. Add to the diplomatic-transcription prompt as explicit examples.

A THIRD engine family (Fable vision on strips) caught errors that survived both single-verifier review AND the two-engine ensemble gate, including a spurious [sic] (metochizeto->metoikizeto: verifier misread kappa as chi, missed the iota subscript, flagged its own misreading as a print anomaly) and a phantom circumflex. N-engine disagreement scales quality; every [sic] must be confirmed by at least two independent engine families before publication.
evidence: fable_idx153.md vs corrected truth: 75 errwords ~= 3-flash's 79, but ~28 are formatting conventions; 2 truth-errors confirmed by eye, ~12 divergences logged open

rule: Scoring needs a transcription-convention spec: superscript note letters (Unicode vs plain), mid-dot spacing, and enclitic double-accents caused ~half of cross-engine 'errors'. Define canonical conventions in the skill prompt and normalize in the scorer.
evidence: 28/59 fable diffs were conventions, not readings
